description: Low-level JSON encode/decode utility (Services_JSON) used across WordPress core and plugins. Deprecated since WP 5.3.0 in favor of the PHP native JSON extension.

use_cases.name: Encode PHP value to JSON string
use_cases.notes: Sets Content-type: application/json header, then delegates to encodeUnsafe(). Returns JSON string or Services_JSON_Error.

use_cases.name: Encode PHP value to JSON without HTTP header (unsafe)
use_cases.notes: Sets LC_NUMERIC locale to 'C' for decimal formatting consistency, calls _encode(). No Content-Type header — XSS risk noted.

use_cases.name: Decode JSON string to PHP value
use_cases.notes: Calls reduce_string() to strip comments/whitespace, then recursively parses JSON tokens into PHP arrays or objects depending on $use flag.

use_cases.name: Serialize object with custom toJSON method
use_cases.notes: When SERVICES_JSON_USE_TO_JSON flag is set and object has toJSON(), calls $var->toJSON() and re-encodes. Guards against infinite recursion; returns Services_JSON_Error if toJSON returns another toJSON-capable object.

use_cases.name: Check if return value is an error
use_cases.notes: Checks if $data is instance of services_json_error or subclass. Falls back to PEAR::isError() if PEAR is present.

use_cases.name: Convert UTF-8 characters during encoding
use_cases.notes: Internal character conversion helpers used by _encode() when processing multi-byte UTF-8 strings. Uses mb_* functions when available.

use_cases.name: Build JSON name-value pair
use_cases.notes: Array-walk callback used during associative array / object encoding to produce '"key":value' fragments.

use_cases.name: Strip JSON comments and whitespace
use_cases.notes: Pre-processing step inside decode(). Removes // line comments, /* */ block comments, and extraneous whitespace before parsing.

use_cases.name: Polyfill global json_encode / json_decode for older PHP
use_cases.notes: compat.php wraps Services_JSON via $wp_json global. Lazy-instantiates Services_JSON if not yet created. Deprecated path; PHP native extension is now required.

key_classes.name: Services_JSON_Error
variants: standalone fallback class (when PEAR not available)
key_classes.purpose: Error object returned instead of throwing exceptions; checked via isError()

call_chain_encode: _encode (array, assoc) → name_value() per key → _encode() per value
call_chain_decode: decode: token-scan loop over reduced string
decode: uses SERVICES_JSON_LOOSE_TYPE flag to return array vs stdClass
key_relationships: Services_JSON uses Services_JSON_Error for error returns
compat.php wraps Services_JSON as global $wp_json polyfill
Services_JSON::isError() delegates to PEAR::isError() if available
WordPress REST API / plugins depend_on Services_JSON (legacy path)
important_notes: Entire file is deprecated since WP 5.3.0; PHP native json_encode/json_decode is now required
encode() sets Content-Type: application/json header — side-effect callers must be aware of
encodeUnsafe() has XSS warning — no header, no escaping for HTML contexts
Strings must be ASCII or UTF-8; other encodings produce '?' substitutions
SERVICES_JSON_LOOSE_TYPE flag controls decode() returning array vs object
Multi-version copies exist: WP 4.8, 5.5, 5.9, 6.1, Wordpress5 dockerfiles — all functionally identical
Use-case diagram should show the legacy polyfill path (compat.php) as a secondary actor flow
